topics.title: Andy Burnham becomes UK prime minister
topics.summary: Andy Burnham has become Britain’s seventh prime minister in a decade after winning the governing Labour Party leadership. He takes over from Keir Starmer while promising a change in direction and standards.
topics.discussionPrompt: When a new leader arrives midstream, what matters more at first: a bold new plan, a calmer tone, or proving they can make everyday systems work?

topics.title: Oil prices swing on conflict and ceasefire hopes
topics.summary: Oil prices briefly reached one-month highs before settling near $88 a barrel. Traders weighed disrupted shipping and wider Middle East tensions against a new proposal for a 10-day ceasefire and renewed U.S.-Iran negotiations.
topics.discussionPrompt: Oil markets can react to both danger and hope in the same afternoon. Do you find that kind of uncertainty fascinating, exhausting, or mostly noticeable when prices reach the gas station?

topics.title: AI-edited bird photos muddy wildlife records
topics.summary: Scientists say AI-enhanced bird photos are introducing false details and sightings into citizen-science databases. Even innocent edits can change a species’ markings, weakening records researchers use to track habitats and migration.
topics.discussionPrompt: If a photo is meant as evidence, should any AI touch-up be clearly labeled, or are harmless edits fine as long as the subject still looks basically right?

topics.title: Australian ski resorts set July heat records
topics.summary: Ski areas in New South Wales and Victoria recorded temperatures up to 12C above average, breaking longstanding July records. Warm, dry weather has left resorts relying heavily on snow-making during a difficult season.
topics.discussionPrompt: If a winter getaway suddenly felt like spring, would you make the best of the strange weather, switch activities, or start rethinking the whole idea of seasonal travel?
